topic.what: integral-resistance property for ciphers with complex linear layers using minimal-transitions method and degree propagation models
topic.how: cell-based MILP degree abstraction, generalized intres framework, heuristic MixColumns optimization via MILP with SAT validation
topic.why_matters: provides strong arguments against integral distinguishers; enables cryptanalysis of low-latency ciphers and design of secure lightweight linear layers

characteristics.what_it_measures: number of rounds with proven integral resistance
characteristics.context: PRINCE cipher with new Mmin MixColumns matrix

characteristics.what_it_measures: number of rounds with proven integral resistance
characteristics.vs_baseline: comparable to exact bounds from prior work

characteristics.what_it_measures: upper bound on minimal degree per round
characteristics.evidence: Table 2 comparison shows tighter cell-based bounds
characteristics.context: improved bounds for rounds 1-11 of Rijndael-256

characteristics.what_it_measures: computation time for MixColumns transition precomputation
characteristics.evidence: algorithmic improvements reduce offline phase drastically
characteristics.context: intres framework vs Zeng-Tian offline phase

characteristics.evidence: heuristic MILP optimization found sparse matrix

characteristics.vs_baseline: 25 ps per round for original PRINCE
characteristics.evidence: 10+5+5=20 ps vs 10+5+2*5=25 ps using NanGate 15nm estimates
characteristics.context: Mmin needs 12 parallel xors vs 2 sequential xor applications

novelty: generalized minimal-transitions method for complex linear layers
heuristic MILP approach for lower-latency MixColumns
first SAT-based validation of minimal-transitions method via monomial trail counting
open_problems: optimizing solely for integral resistance may affect other security
extension to tweakey schedules not yet explored
ciphers without max-degree full-rank S-Boxes need further investigation
scalability to larger cipher states remains limited
